sample.title: PrimerClip4_ObservedCausation: To what extent do you agree with the following statement: Marble B went through the gate, because marble A hit marble B.

code.parse_mode_reason: Requested 2D physics subset pass; 3D lifting is forbidden for this run.

readme_task_contract.summary: Marble omission clips; marbles are dynamic, gate/wall/barrier is context.

queries.prompt:
To what extent do you agree with the following statement:

Marble B went through the gate, because marble A hit marble B.

attached_trials.prompt:
To what extent do you agree with the following statement:

Marble B went through the gate, because marble A hit marble B.

scene.description: PrimerClip4: Marble A hits marble B; B goes through the gate (causation scenario)
scene.world: 2-marble setup: marble A (causal agent) hits marble B (target); gate on left wall
scene.note: Exact initial positions/velocities for primer clips not provided in trial_config.json; primer clips are for instruction purposes

query.prompt:
To what extent do you agree with the following statement:

Marble B went through the gate, because marble A hit marble B.

queries.prompt: What do you think are the chances that Marble B would have missed the gate if Marble A had not hit it?

attached_trials.prompt: What do you think are the chances that Marble B would have missed the gate if Marble A had not hit it?

scene.description: PrimerClip4: Marble A hits marble B; B goes through the gate (causation scenario)
scene.world: 2-marble setup: marble A (causal agent) hits marble B (target); gate on left wall

query.prompt: What do you think are the chances that Marble B would have missed the gate if Marble A had not hit it?

config_metadata.experimentName: A counterfactual simulation model of causation by omission (Exp 1)
config_metadata.description: You will watch short video clips showing two marbles on a playing field with a gate. After watching each clip, you will answer a question about what you observed.
